key_insight: A transfer-learning and loss-function upgrade to BiSeNetV2 substantially improves reflective weld seam segmentation without making the model heavier.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical real-time perception boost for autonomous welding robots operating on shiny metal surfaces, where seam detection is often unstable.
method: Start with a BiSeNetV2 lightweight segmentation backbone.
Apply transfer learning to stabilize learning on challenging reflective seam data.
Use a hybrid Cross-Entropy + Lovász loss to improve boundary and IoU performance.
evidence_signal: The method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameter count, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on reflective construction-welding conditions and should be validated across more seam types, sensors, and deployment environments.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
